meta.purpose: Encode knowledge management judgment so AI distinguishes between raw materials, organized information, and genuine knowledge assets — and helps users identify what their knowledge base is missing.
meta.load_condition: Load when the user asks about organizing notes, evaluating knowledge bases, managing PKM systems, or when AI is acting on a collection of saved materials.

axioms.one_sentence: Most saved material is unprocessed raw material, not a knowledge asset.
axioms.full_statement: A knowledge asset must have: a core proposition (what this material claims), an applicability boundary (when it does and does not apply), at least one counter-example (when it fails), and an evolution record (how the understanding changed). Most saved notes, bookmarks, and clipped articles meet zero of these criteria. They are raw material, not knowledge.
axioms.why: Agents treating all saved content as equally valuable knowledge produce the illusion of a knowledge base while the user has only accumulated information. The agent must distinguish between collected information and processed knowledge.
applies_when: Curating a personal knowledge management system where users bulk-import web clippings and PDFs without annotation.
Indexing a user's saved browser bookmarks and email drafts for AI retrieval and summarization.
Archiving corporate meeting recordings and slide decks intended for long-term strategic reference.
does_not_apply_when: Storing legal contracts, tax records, or compliance logs where verbatim raw storage is legally mandated.
Maintaining reference tables, API documentation, or standard operating procedures that are authoritative and context-independent.
Archiving personal photos, voice memos, or creative drafts where the raw artifact itself holds the primary value.
axioms.failure_risk: The agent would incorrectly strip or ignore authoritative reference documents and legal records by labeling them unprocessed, causing critical data loss or compliance failures.

axioms.one_sentence: The structure of saved knowledge is not an organizational preference — it determines whether the knowledge can be reused.
axioms.full_statement: A flat list of notes about 'leadership' has low reuse value regardless of how many notes exist. The structure that enables reuse includes: what scenario this knowledge applies to, what signal triggers it, what it contradicts, and what result it has produced before.
axioms.why: Agents optimizing for 'more links' or 'better tags' are optimizing for retrieval, not for reusability. Retrieval finds the note. Reusability means the note changes the agent's judgment when found.
applies_when: Building decision-support knowledge graphs for clinical diagnosis or engineering troubleshooting workflows.
Organizing post-mortem reports and incident responses to trigger relevant lessons during live system alerts.
Curating sales enablement playbooks that require explicit win conditions, competitor counter-moves, and boundary constraints.
does_not_apply_when: Maintaining a corporate FAQ or technical glossary where direct keyword lookup provides complete unambiguous answers.
Storing static media assets like logos, presentation templates, or boilerplate code snippets requiring mechanical copy-paste reuse.
Archiving chronological personal journals or reflective essays where narrative flow is the sole objective.
axioms.failure_risk: The agent would waste computational cycles forcing unnecessary scenario metadata onto exact-match reference items, degrading search performance and user trust.

axioms.one_sentence: Knowledge assets decay when they are not tested against new situations.
axioms.full_statement: A knowledge asset that was accurate in one context may become misleading in another. Without an evolution record — when it was created, what situations have tested it, what counter-examples have emerged — it becomes noise dressed as insight.
axioms.why: Agents treat static knowledge as permanently valid. Real expertise evolves through falsification, not accumulation. The agent should flag knowledge assets without an evolution record as unverified.
applies_when: Managing market analysis reports, competitive intelligence briefs, or financial forecasting models.
Maintaining clinical treatment guidelines, safety protocols, or regulatory compliance frameworks.
Curating software architecture patterns and engineering best practices that shift with new frameworks and hardware.
does_not_apply_when: Preserving historical facts, mathematical theorems, or fundamental physics laws that are empirically stable.
Storing immutable signed agreements, statutory compliance checklists, or enacted legislation text.
Archiving verified cultural heritage records, historical timelines, or standardized unit conversion charts.
axioms.failure_risk: The agent would incorrectly downgrade immutable reference standards and historical facts as decaying or unverified, introducing unnecessary friction and discarding foundational truths.

ontology.one_sentence: A unit of knowledge that meets four criteria: core proposition, applicability boundary, counter-example, and evolution record.
ontology.essence: It is not a saved web page, a highlighted passage, or a summarized concept. It is a claim about how something works — paired with when it does not work, what would prove it wrong, and how the understanding has changed over time.
ontology.boundary: It is not a bookmark, a note, a summary, a tag collection, or a mind map. These are containers. A knowledge asset is the judgment inside the container. Most containers are empty.
ontology.trigger_signal: The user shows a note-taking system with many items but each item is a short description, a link, or a one-sentence summary — no proposition, no boundary, no counter-example.

ontology.one_sentence: Saved content that has not been processed into a knowledge asset.
ontology.essence: Raw material is anything the user has captured — articles, quotes, ideas, screenshots, tweets, meeting notes — that has not been tested for proposition, boundary, counter-example, and evolution.
ontology.boundary: It is not useless. Raw material is the input to knowledge creation. But treating raw material as knowledge is the most common failure in personal knowledge management.
ontology.trigger_signal: The user says 'I have 500 notes on this topic' but cannot articulate the core proposition or boundary of any single note.

frameworks.name: Knowledge Asset Test
frameworks.when_to_use: Use when evaluating whether saved content qualifies as a knowledge asset, or when helping a user turn raw material into knowledge.
steps: Identify the core proposition: what specific claim does this content make about how something works?
Identify the boundary: under what conditions does this claim break or not apply?
Identify at least one counter-example: a real situation where following this claim would produce a worse result.
Identify the evolution record: when was this understanding formed, and how has it changed?
If any of the four is missing, classify as raw material and explain what is needed to promote it.

core_structure.via: testing each item for proposition, boundary, counter-example, and evolution — most will fail
checking whether finding the note changes the agent's judgment or just surfaces the note
checking for evolution record — if none exists, the knowledge may already be wrong for current context

stances: A small collection of tested knowledge assets is worth more than a large collection of unprocessed raw material.
When evaluating a knowledge base, count knowledge assets, not notes. If the user cannot name the proposition and boundary of a note, it is raw material.
Do not suggest better organization as a substitute for better processing. Reorganizing raw material does not create knowledge.
